title: DSDS Guide Definitions
description: Defines documents for long-form, reading-oriented guidance that is not tied to a single component, token, foundation, or pattern. A guide covers getting-started walkthroughs, contribution instructions, tutorials, conceptual overviews, migration guides, and any other narrative documentation a design system needs. A guide document holds identity and metadata; the kind of guide (getting-started, contribution, tutorial, and so on) is carried by a `category` metadata entry, keeping the entity flexible enough for any guideline. Typed entries in the `metadata` array carry modular metadata. Typed entries in the `documentBlocks` array carry structured docs — narrative sections, procedures, guidelines, and use-cases.

guide.description: A long-form, reading-oriented document: a getting-started walkthrough, a contribution guide, a tutorial, a conceptual overview, a migration guide, or any narrative guidance the system needs. Guides fill the gap left by the artifact-bound entities (component, token, foundation, pattern), which document a single thing rather than a journey or a concept. The top level holds identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` array holds modular entries with a `kind` tag — use a `category` entry (e.g., 'getting-started', 'contribution', 'tutorial', 'concept', 'migration') to classify the guide, and `tags` for finer-grained discovery. The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries: section (narrative prose), steps (procedures), guideline, use-cases, import, accessibility, and content.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a guide.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the guide (e.g., 'getting-started', 'contributing', 'theming-tutorial', 'migrating-to-v2'). MUST be lowercase kebab-case and unique within the documentation group.

name.description: Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Getting started', 'Contributing', 'Theming tutorial', 'Migrating to v2').

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` tag. Kinds include description, status, since, last-updated, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. Use a `category` entry to classify the guide (e.g., 'getting-started', 'contribution', 'tutorial', 'concept', 'migration').

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this guide, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Accepts the guide-specific kinds (section, steps) plus reused kinds (import for setup instructions, guideline, use-cases, accessibility, content). The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display, since a guide reads top to bottom. Express links to other entities and external resources through a `links` metadata entry or a section's `links`, not as document block entries.

agents.description: AI-ready context for this guide: intent, constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords for efficient AI use.
